Full_JD_Text: About the internship Selected intern's day-to-day responsibilities include: 4.1  Application Development and Engineering Design, build, and maintain AI-enabled and geospatial software applications spanning desktop, web, and cloud platforms. Contribute across the full technology stack, including user interfaces, back-end services, and automated data pipelines. Support enterprise-grade mobile, desktop, and web products through the complete software lifecycle   from initial development through deployment, quality assurance, and ongoing maintenance. 4.2  AI Agent Development Assist in the design, development, training, testing, and iterative refinement of AI agents for international development applications. Integrate AI tools and large language model (LLM) capabilities into existing geospatial software platforms used across development programs. 4.3  Data Infrastructure and Cloud Design and build structured and unstructured data pipelines optimized for geospatial data at scale. Integrate storage and database solutions, including PostgreSQL/PostGIS, AWS S3, and key-value stores. Support deployment of applications and machine learning models on cloud platforms, ensuring reliability, scalability, and security. 4.4  Collaboration and Knowledge Management Collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams, subject matter experts, consultants, and project managers across multiple regions and time zones. Participate in code reviews and provide mentorship and technical guidance to junior developers and fellow interns. Maintain comprehensive documentation of technical workflows, development processes, and established best practices. Prepare clear and concise technical notes, meeting minutes, and progress summaries for internal and external audiences. 5  Required Qualifications Currently enrolled in the final year of a Bachelor's degree programme in Computer Science, Data Science, or a closely related discipline. A minimum of one year of academic study and applied experience in AI for software products, including LLM integration, prompt and tool orchestration, model evaluation, or ML pipeline development and deployment. Strong software engineering fundamentals, including version control (Git), testing practices, debugging methodologies, and the ability to design and maintain scalable, well-structured systems. Proficiency in Python for application development and data processing; familiarity with UI frameworks (e.g., PyQt5) and/or back-end services (e.g., Node.js, FastAPI, or equivalent). Excellent written and verbal English communication skills, with demonstrated ability to articulate technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Ability to work from our Rohini, Delhi office a minimum of two days per week (three days preferred). This requirement is non-negotiable. 6  Preferred Skills and Experience Demonstrated experience integrating LLMs and tool-calling APIs (e.g., OpenAI API, Azure OpenAI), including prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and systematic model evaluation. Hands-on experience with LLM and agent orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex) and vector search solutions (e.g., pgvector, Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus, Elasticsearch/OpenSearch). Practical knowledge of ML/MLOps workflows and deployment tooling (e.g., MLflow, DVC, Airflow, Prefect, Docker, Kubernetes) and model serving infrastructure (e.g., FastAPI, TorchServe, Triton Inference Server). Familiarity with AI quality assurance, safety, and observability practices, including evaluation frameworks, robustness testing, cost and latency optimization, and monitoring tools such as LangSmith, Arize/Phoenix, or OpenTelemetry. Working knowledge of machine learning frameworks such as TensorFlow or PyTorch, with experience applying them in research or applied project contexts. You'll work closely with multiple teams to  automate manual workflows, improve efficiency, and build lightweight AI-powered systems  that save time and scale impact. What You'll Do - Identify repetitive or manual tasks across teams and design AI-driven solutions to automate them. - Experiment with and implement AI tools (LLMs, no-code/low-code tools, agents, APIs) to improve productivity. - Build simple internal tools, workflows, or scripts that reduce human effort. - Work with teams to understand problems, prototype solutions, and iterate quickly. Document workflows, automations, and learnings so others can use and scale them. - Stay updated with new AI tools, frameworks, and best practices and proactively suggest improvements. Who You Are - Recently graduated or final-year student in any field (engineering background is a plus, but not mandatory). - Strong interest in  AI tools and applied AI use-cases  (ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, Zapier, Make, Notion AI, etc.). - Comfortable experimenting, breaking things, and figuring stuff out independently. - High ownership mindset     you don't wait for perfect instructions. - Able to think in workflows and systems, not just one-off tasks. - Basic scripting or technical comfort (Python, JavaScript, APIs, spreadsheets, or automation tools) is a plus. What We're Looking For - Someone who can  "play with AI" productively      test tools, connect systems, and ship usable solutions. - Practical mindset:  "How can this save 5-10 hours a week for the team?" - Strong problem-solving and communication skills. - Willingness to learn fast and adapt in a high-ownership environment. Why This Role Is Exciting - Hands-on exposure to  real-world AI implementation, not theoretical work. - Opportunity to work across teams and functions. - High impact  your work directly improves team efficiency. - Steep learning curve and mentorship in a fast-moving environment. - Potential for conversion to a full-time role based on performance. Conduct structured A/B testing of prompts and document findings to optimize conversation quality. 6. Curate and label datasets while helping build evaluation sets to measure AI performance objectively. 7. Monitor the live performance of Voice AI agents, identify issues, and support the timely resolution of production problems. 8. Maintain prompt libraries, operational playbooks, and standard operating procedures (SOPs) to ensure consistency and knowledge sharing. 9. Manage day-to-day operational tasks, including issue triaging, escalation handling, and incorporating customer feedback into product improvements. Full_JD_Text: About part time job/internship Cortex DB is building the AI-native database of the future     rethinking how applications store, query, and reason over data in a world where AI agents are the primary consumers of information. We're an early-stage, high-conviction team and we're moving fast. The company was founded by a team from IIT Delhi, including an ex-PM from Flipkart, repeat founders, and serial entrepreneurs     and is led by Founder Prashant Malik, co-creator of Apache Cassandra, the distributed database that powers some of the world's largest technology companies. Prashant is a Web 2.0 veteran who has seen infrastructure revolutions from the inside, and he's building Cortex DB to be the foundational data layer for the AI era. If you want to learn what it actually takes to build a category-defining developer tool from zero to one, this is the room to be in. The Opportunity: The AI and  agentic space is exploding with new companies, new use cases, new infrastructure every week. Bangalore is at the center of it. Conferences, meetups, and developer events are happening constantly, and this role puts you right in the middle of it all. We're looking for someone who is already showing up to these events     not because they have to, but because they genuinely want to understand where AI is going. If that's you, we want to make those trips count. You'll be our eyes and ears on the ground, the person who connects Cortex DB with the engineers and teams building the next wave of AI applications and in the process, get an unfiltered education in how a world-class team does early-stage customer acquisition for a developer tools product. Selected intern's day-to-day responsibilities include: 1. Spread the word about Cortex DB: Show up at AI, agentic, and developer-focused conferences and events across Bangalore and get people excited about what we're building. Hand out materials, strike up conversations, and leave people with a clear sense of who Cortex DB is and why it matters. 2. Talk to people     genuinely: The core of this role is human connection. Chat with engineers, founders, and product builders. Ask them what they're working on. Share what Cortex DB is doing. You don't need a script     just curiosity and the ability to hold a good conversation. 3. Gather feedback along the way: If someone has thoughts on what we're building, capture it. A quick note on what people reacted to, what questions came up, or what they found interesting is genuinely valuable to the team. Low pressure     just keep your ears open. 4. Be a connector: If you meet someone who would clearly benefit from Cortex DB, flag it to the team. No expectation to close anything     just make the introduction and let us take it from there. What you'll walk away with: 1. A front-row seat to how a world-class founding team builds a developer tools company from zero. 2. Real experience in early-stage GTM, customer discovery, and funnel-building     the kind that translates to product, sales, and growth roles as the company scales. 3. Direct access to the founding team     founders, ex-PMs, and operators who've done this before and will invest time in helping you grow. 4. Meaningful work that actually matters     your conversations will shape Cortex DB's product roadmap and first customer relationships. 5. A path to expanded responsibility within the company as adoption grows. Stipend:    500-   800 per event (based on duration) + travel reimbursements. Duration: Flexible     per-event basis, with strong potential for ongoing engagement. Location: Bangalore (field-based, across conference venues in the city). Start date: Immediate. Skill(s) required Computer skills Effective Communication English Proficiency (Spoken) English Proficiency (Written) Interpersonal skills Technical Analysis Earn certifications in these skills Learn Effective Communication Learn Business Communication Learn Interpersonal skills Who can apply Only those candidates can apply who: 1. are available for the part time job/internship 2. can start the part time job/internship between 17th Jun'26 and 22nd Jul'26 3. are available for duration of 3 months 4. have relevant skills and interests * Women wanting to start/restart their career can also apply. Other requirements Requirements: 1. Undergrad or postgrad student in CS, IT, Business, or a related field — or an early-career graduate looking to get real exposure to the AI and developer tools space. 2. A genuine interest in AI, agentic systems, or the developer ecosystem — if you're already going to conferences and meetups in this space, that's a big plus, but not a requirement. 3. Someone who enjoys talking to people and can hold a natural, thoughtful conversation — you don't need to be a salesperson, just someone who communicates well. 4. Comfortable representing a brand in public settings with warmth and energy. 5. Curious enough to ask good questions and observe what's happening around you. A note on skills: 1. If you haven't done this kind of work before, that's completely fine. 2. We'll help you get comfortable with how to talk to users, what to listen for, and how to represent Cortex DB in a way that feels natural to you. 3. Think of it as learning a real-world skill set — user conversations, feedback gathering, community presence — that will serve you well in any product, growth, or business role going forward.
